key_insight: Off-Context GRPO uses privileged guided rollouts during training, then corrects the update back to the original unguided objective so models can learn on hard reasoning problems even when standard RLVR gets zero signal.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical way to improve mathematical reasoning in large language models without changing the base architecture, adding only minimal training overhead.
method: Generate rollouts with privileged guidance, such as solution prefixes, to obtain non-zero rewards on difficult tasks.
Treat these as off-context: the rollout is guided, but the optimization target remains the original prompt without guidance.
Apply an importance-corrected GRPO objective to reduce train-test mismatch and stabilize learning.
evidence_signal: Across standard mathematical reasoning benchmarks, the method reports a 3.9% absolute improvement, or 13.8% relative gain, over vanilla GRPO with negligible additional cost.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the availability and quality of privileged guidance, and guided training must be carefully corrected to avoid objective mismatch.
paper_title: Off-Context GRPO: Learning to Reason on Hard Problems using Privileged Information
